original_abstract: It is impossible for humans to carry out their role as bearers of the mandate of Allah SWT in this world without communication. Likewise, every parent should talk to their children from childhood, especially about matters related to religious values. Religion functions as identity, a moral source, a guide to truth, and even spiritual guidance for humans. Religious education in the family is very important and mandatory because it will influence children's morality so that they do not fall into promiscuity which is dangerous for their future. Children at an early age experience rapid physical and mental growth. It is very important to encourage children to learn because of this rapid development and growth. This research is research that uses qualitative methods with data collection using interview and documentation techniques. The results of this research are: In the psychology paradigm, there are at least 4 theories related to early childhood development, including social, cognitive and constructivist theories: Social Cognitive Theory, teaches the idea that the social environment is where most human learning occurs. Furthermore, they act according to their beliefs about their abilities and the expected outcomes of their actions. Cognitive Development Theory. teaches that the thought process a person has, which includes their ability to relate, assess, and consider what happens or happens to them. There are 4 stages of cognitive development based on age: a. Sensory (sensory motor) stage, b. Preoperational stage (preoperational), c. concrete operational stage (concrete operational), d. formal operational stage (formal operational), Constructivism theory is a learning theory that aims to encourage students to actively discover and build their own knowledge and improve their abilities and skills independently.
humanized_abstract: For humans to hold the responsibility to be a representative of Allah SWT in this world there has to be a communication. This is where as a parent, every human being has to communicate or speak to the child from the earliest age of childhood especially on matters concerning Islam. Islam is an identity of mankind, as well as their source of ethics and their guide to haq (truth). As an identity, the religion has a great influence over the mankind, which then acts as their spiritual guidance towards achieving Allah SWT’s wish in their lives. The Islamic education in the family is of great significant because it helps the children to increase their sense of morality so that would ward them from indecent and prohibited acts and behaviour, which might jeopardize their future in this life and hereafter. Children go through tremendous development growth both physically and mentally at very early ages of their lives. This makes it important that children are guided to learn from an early stage in their lives. Therefore, this study is based on qualitative approach and used interviews and documentation methods for data collection. Some of the findings from this study were: From the psychology perspective there are four developmental theories in early childhood, namely: i. Social Development Theory states that the main place where humans learn is from their social environment. They behave based on what they believe is their capability to do things or the outcomes of the things they do. ii. Cognitive Development Theory explain the process of thinking or assessing something which enables them to link, evaluate and judge various things around them. Based on age there are four cognitive developmental stages which are: a. Stage of sensorimotor (sensory), b. stage of preoperational, c. stage of concrete operational and d. stage of formal operational. iv. Constructivism theory is a learning theory in which teachers persuade students to do their own discover and construction of knowledge and also to develop their abilities and skills by doing things independently on their own.
